summary: Gruber announces the return of Daring Fireball t-shirts due to remaining stock from the first run and continued reader demand. The shirts are priced at $29 and include a one-year membership to the site. He outlines shipping details, notes that ladies' sizes are no longer available, and warns that stock is first-come, first-served with a second printing run planned. Existing members can purchase shirts at a significant discount. The post is a straightforward commerce announcement tied to his membership-driven business model.
key_points: Daring Fireball t-shirts are back due to leftover stock and ongoing reader requests
Each shirt purchase includes a one-year Daring Fireball membership with perks like members-only RSS feeds
Existing members get a steep discount on shirts, rewarding early supporters
The membership model is Gruber's approach to sustaining independent publishing
money_quotes: Current stock will be shipped first-come, first-served.
Everyone who purchases a shirt also gets a one-year membership to Daring Fireball.
To everyone who's already become a member: my sincere thanks.
key_insight: Gruber uses merchandise as both a revenue stream and a membership incentive, tying physical goods to his independent publishing business model.
